steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Barragán traveled to the US and Europe in the early 1930s and then began incorporating "functionalism and modernism" in later work — visible in the Avenida Parque México houses with their "unadorned geometric forms." The Calle Liceo house is described as an "earlier project" that shows "the aesthetics of traditional Mediterranean and Mexican styles." The contrast is explicit: traditional/ornate earlier vs. unadorned/geometric later. The correct answer must reflect only what the passage actually states about Calle Liceo.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It represents a transitional moment between the early and late phases of Barragán's development.": the passage puts Calle Liceo squarely in the early phase, not between phases. ✗
  • "It displays the effects of Barragán's exposure to international architectural trends in the 1930s.": those international trends influenced the later Avenida Parque México houses, not Calle Liceo. ✗
  • "It reflects an approach to ornamentation and shape that Barragán later stopped using.": Calle Liceo shows "traditional Mediterranean and Mexican styles" (ornamentation); Barragán later switched to "unadorned geometric forms" (stopped using that approach). Matches exactly. ✓
  • "It is characteristic of the Guadalajaran architecture that influenced Barragán throughout his career.": the passage says the traditional style is an earlier phase he moved away from, not a lifelong influence. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
